steps.title: Compare moon distance
steps.content: Text 1 imagines the moon as a child's balloon tangled in a mast, making something that seemed far away appear close. Text 2 repeatedly emphasizes distance with up so high and the request for the moon to come down.

steps.title: Reject misread contrasts
steps.content: Choice A wrongly treats both moons as entrapped. Choice B says only Text 1 addresses beauty, which is not its focus. Choice C misreads Text 2's affectionate address as serious study. The answer is D.
